title: orostachys aggregata var. boehmeri
intro: Orostachys aggregata var. boehmeri, also known as the Tibetan sedum, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the Crassulaceae family, native to the high-altitude regions of the Tibetan Plateau. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Orostachys aggregata var. boehmeri include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
